mechanism_narrative: Persistent low federal funds rate signals accommodative monetary policy, which reduces the cost of capital for businesses and encourages investment, leading to positive equity market performance over the short term.
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS remains low but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently negative or flat (e.g., average return < 0.05%) over a 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate signals a weakening labor market and potential economic contraction, which typically leads to lower corporate earnings expectations and negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by the specified trigger but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: High and accelerating consumer price inflation combined with robust retail sales indicates strong consumer demand but also potential for aggressive monetary tightening, which creates uncertainty and pressures corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL annual growth > 5% AND RSAFS annual growth > 5%
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 287.708 as of t-1mo (high inflation)
fact:RSAFS at 665726.0 as of t-1mo (strong retail sales)
failure_condition: If both CPIAUCSL and RSAFS meet their growth triggers, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant deceleration in industrial production growth during a period of high inflation signals supply-side constraints and potential stagflation, which negatively impacts corporate profitability and leads to lower equity valuations over the very short term.
failure_condition: If INDPRO growth decelerates as specified during high inflation, but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 50% of such instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts indicates a significant slowdown in construction and broader economic activity, reflecting weakening consumer and business confidence, which typically precedes or accompanies negative equity market performance over the medium term.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls by more than 15% month-over-month
failure_condition: If HOUST declines by the specified trigger, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in non-farm payrolls signifies a healthy and expanding labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate revenues, which generally translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent growth (e.g., > 100k jobs/month) but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative on average over any 12-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate combined with slowing GDP growth indicates a tightening monetary policy impacting a weakening economy, which typically leads to a re-evaluation of equity valuations downwards as discount rates rise and earnings prospects dim over the longer term.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by 0.25% in a month AND GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 2%
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS rises and GDPC1 growth slows as specified, but S&P 500 60-day returns are positive in more than 40% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: High and persistent inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes purchasing power and corporate profit margins, prompting central bank tightening and leading to negative sentiment and lower equity prices over the very short term, especially in a high-inflation regime.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 287.708 as of t-1mo (high inflation)
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 6% but S&P 500 5-day returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances over a 5-year period.
